jobs.title: Data Analyst I, II, or III - TPP Division - Austin, TX
jobs.description:
Data Analyst I:

Performs entry-level data analysis and data research work. Work involves conducting detailed analysis and extensive research on

data and providing results in various formats. Responsible for using standard statistical tools, methods and techniques to

translate data into actionable insights using data visualization software. Works under close supervision, with minimal latitude

for the use of initiative and independent judgment.

Data Analyst II:

Performs routine data analysis and data research work. Work involves conducting detailed analysis and extensive research on data and providing results in various formats. Responsible for using standard statistical tools, methods and techniques to translate data into actionable insights using data visualization software. Works under moderate supervision, with limited latitude for the use of initiative and independent judgment.

Data Analyst III:

Performs moderately complex data analysis and data research work. Work involves conducting detailed analysis and extensive research on data and providing results in various formats. Monitors and implements measures to ensure data quality. Responsible for using standard statistical tools, methods and techniques to translate data into actionable insights using data visualization software. Works under general supervision, with limited latitude for the use of initiative and independent judgment.

Essential Duties:

Data Analyst I:

Compiles and queries data.
Analyzes data using standard statistical tools, methods, and techniques
Interprets results to identify significant differences in data.
Cleans and prunes data to discard irrelevant information.
Assists in defining, developing, and implementing data standards.
Writes programs and/or queries for spreadsheet or database software to create reports and import/export data.
Assists in Identifying and interpreting data patterns and trends and assessing data quality.
Performs other job responsibilities as assigned.

Data Analyst II:

All the Above and:

Modifies existing reports and/or develops new reports by analyzing databases, data sources, and structures for the benefit of users

Data Analyst III:

All the Above and:

Prepares concise, comprehensive technical reports to present and interpret data, identify alternatives, and make and justify recommendations on data revisions.

Minimum Qualifications:
Data Analyst I:
Education:

Bachelor's Degree in a related discipline.
Relevant experience may be substituted for education on a year per year basis. Substitutions for Minimum Qualifications

Experience:

None
Experience can be satisfied by full-time or prorated part-time equivalent

Licenses and Certifications:

Valid driver’s license. This position requires driving a state vehicle.

Data Analyst II:
Education:

Bachelor's Degree in a related discipline.
Relevant experience may be substituted for education on a year per year basis. Substitutions for Minimum Qualifications OR related graduate level education may be substituted for experience on a year per year basis.

Experience:

1 YEAR data analysis, research, compilation, and/or report writing and design work experience.
Experience can be satisfied by full-time or prorated part-time equivalent

Licenses and Certifications:

Valid driver’s license. This position requires driving a state vehicle.

Data Analyst III:
Education:

Bachelor's Degree in a related discipline.
Relevant experience may be substituted for education on a year per year basis. Substitutions for Minimum Qualifications OR related graduate level education may be substituted for experience on a year per year basis.

Experience:

2 YEAR data analysis, research, compilation, and/or report writing and design work experience.
Experience can be satisfied by full-time or prorated part-time equivalent

Licenses and Certifications:

Valid driver’s license. This position requires driving a state vehicle.

Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Transportation Planning, Transportation Engineering preferred

Competencies:

Data Analyst I, II, or III:

Data analysis and interpretation
Analyze data and derive informative conclusions.
Evaluate and make recommendations regarding data integrity
Effectively organize and prioritize work demands in a fast paced environment
Translate complex data into user friendly information
Exercises logic and reasoning to define problems, establish facts and draw valid conclusions; makes decisions that support business objectives and goals
Business processes and methodologies, data import and export protocols
Communicating technical information effectively
Working with streaming data and/or unstructured data from multiple data sources
Developing and presenting reports in multiple formats

Physical Requirements and Working Conditions:

Sedentary work: Lift up to 10 lbs at a time and occasionally carry files/small tools
Sitting - prolonged periods of time
Fingering-picking, pinching, typing; working primarily with fingers rather than whole hand
Close Visual Acuity-work includes data/figures; view a computer screen; extensive reading

Conditions of Employment:

Please read the Standard Conditions of Employment (TxDOT) for all positions.

Position Information

Traffic Section of TPP is looking for a Traffic Monitoring Data Processor to collaborate on the validation and reporting of TxDOT traffic data and statistics. Entry level or experienced Traffic Monitoring and Highway Performance Monitoring System (HPMS) data professionals may be a good fit for this role. We are looking for individuals who are interested in traffic volume statistics, enjoy working with large amounts of traffic data, and who have experience processing data using the Statewide Analysis and Reporting System II (STARS II), or who are willing to learn this system.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Data Analyst III

Location:
New York, NY (Hybrid – 3 Days Onsite at Hudson Yards)
Duration:
9 Months
Start Date:
ASAP
Accepting candidates who are authorised to work in United states without requiring any sponsorship.

About the Role

Seeking a Data Analyst III to join the Client Data Services (CDS) organization. The role will work closely with Data Analysts, Product Managers, Data Scientists, Software Engineers, and Data Engineers to optimize growth strategies, improve data quality, and deliver actionable business insights. This position offers an opportunity to solve complex data challenges while directly influencing business growth and customer support initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Collaborate with Product Managers, Engineers, and Data Scientists to understand business requirements and deliver actionable insights.
Develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines, ETL processes, and data models.
Extract, analyze, and interpret large datasets using SQL, Python, and related technologies.
Design and maintain dashboards and reporting solutions to support business decision-making.
Ensure data quality, privacy, security, compliance, and operational excellence across assigned data domains.
Support monetization, contact ability, and client data initiatives through analytics and reporting.
Partner with offshore teams to manage and process data change requests efficiently.
Provide recommendations and strategic insights to support business growth and operational improvements.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related technical field.
5+ years of experience in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence roles.
Strong SQL expertise with experience querying large datasets and non-relational databases.

Job Title:
Data Analyst (Looker, SQL, BigQuery)
Location:
Remote (US Only)
Department:
Data Analytics
Reports To:
Director of Data Operations
Job Summary:

At Quote.com, we're a tech-enabled omnichannel performance marketing organization that delivers high-quality, mission-critical demand at scale to industry-leading partners. We serve customers across multiple market verticals including Auto, Health, Home, and Life Insurance, providing significant value to consumers through an independent, third-party comparison-shopping experience.
We're seeking a Data Analyst to turn SQL into dashboards for actionable insights that drive revenue optimization, partner performance, and strategic decision-making across the organization. You'll build Looker dashboards, write SQL, and deliver the reporting that marketing across the organization rely on daily.
This is a hands-on role for someone who is strong in SQL and Looker and eager to grow their skills while delivering analytics that matter. You'll work directly with marketing, operations, and engineering teams to surface opportunities, monitor KPIs, and ensure data-driven decisions at every level.
Key Responsibilities:

Build and maintain Looker dashboards and explores that provide real-time visibility into lead generation, monetization, and partner performance metrics.
Write complex SQL queries in BigQuery to analyze large datasets spanning insurance leads, call routing, partner revenue, and marketing attribution.
Develop and maintain LookML models that accurately represent business logic and enable self-service analytics across teams.
Analyze campaign performance, conversion funnels, and revenue trends to identify optimization opportunities.
Create automated reporting workflows that replace manual processes and ensure stakeholders have timely, accurate data.
Partner with marketing and operations teams to define KPIs, set targets, and build the measurement frameworks to track them.
Perform ad hoc analysis to support strategic initiatives, pricing decisions, and partner negotiations with data-backed recommendations.
Monitor data quality within analytics outputs, flagging discrepancies and working with data engineering to resolve upstream issues.
Document data definitions, metric calculations, and dashboard logic to ensure consistency and institutional knowledge.

Must-Have Technical Skills:

Hands-on expertise with:
Looker & LookML:
Building dashboards and explores; experience working with and contributing to LookML models
SQL:
Strong SQL proficiency, comfortable with window functions, CTEs, complex joins, and subqueries in BigQuery
BigQuery:
Experience querying and analyzing large-scale datasets in Google BigQuery
Data Visualization:
Ability to design clear dashboards that communicate insights to technical and non-technical audiences
Data Analysis:
Can take a business question and turn it into a structured analysis

You should have experience delivering analytics in a Data Analyst
, Business Analyst
, or BI Analyst
capacity.
Nice-to-Have Skills:

Experience or familiarity with any of the following:
Transformation Tools:
DBT for managing SQL-based transformation layers
Spreadsheets:
Advanced Google Sheets or Excel for modeling and ad hoc analysis
Python/R:
Scripting for data analysis, automation, or statistical modeling
Performance Marketing:
Familiarity with lead generation, CPA/CPL metrics, and insurance vertical dynamics
Databases:
Familiarity with StratoBaseDB or similar columnar analytics databases
ETL/Data Pipelines:
Understanding of how data flows from source systems into the warehouse
Version Control:
Git and GitHub for managing LookML and analytics code
Statistical Analysis:
A/B testing, regression analysis, or forecasting techniques

Qualifications & Experience:

2+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or analytics role.
Strong Looker experience including building dashboards, explores, and working with LookML models.
Strong SQL skills with the ability to write complex queries against large datasets.
Experience working with BigQuery or similar cloud data warehouses.
Ability to translate business questions into clear, structured analyses.
Good communication skills. You can present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Comfortable working autonomously in a fast-paced, remote environment.
Experience in performance marketing, insurance, or lead generation is a plus.

What We Offer:

Compensation:
Salary + Annual Performance-based Bonus
Benefits:

401(k) with a competitive company match
Health insurance, including HSA option
Dental and vision coverage
Life and AD&D insurance

Work-Life Balance:
Fully remote company with flexible schedules to support balance and wellness
Growth Opportunities:
Potential to expand scope into analytics engineering, data strategy, or team leadership
Team:
A small, collaborative team that values autonomy and getting things done
How to Apply:

Submit your resume and a short note explaining why this role excites you and how your experience with Looker and SQL-driven analytics makes you a great fit.

To support client needs, candidates must reside within a two-hour drive of Massachusetts. While this position is primarily remote, occasional onsite visits will be required.

Position Overview

We are seeking a highly organized and progressive Data Analyst to serve as a key partner to the Transmission Line Engineering team. This individual will act as a right-hand resource, helping to elevate team performance through structured data, reporting, and actionable team and individual insights. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building systems from the ground up, improving processes, and translating complex data into clear, visual insights that drive decision making.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain KPI dashboards to track team and project performance
Build and refine reporting frameworks to support transmission engineering operations
Perform back testing and data validation to ensure accuracy and reliability of metrics

Lead Power BI dashboard development and integration across engineering workflows

Experience building KPIs, dashboards, and reporting systems

Excellent communication skills and ability to present insights clearly

Engineering team has clear, reliable dashboards to track performance

jobs.title: Business Analytics and Recognition Data Analyst
jobs.description:
We are a Firm where people truly believe in what they do and strive to achieve the highest standards of performance and success.

This position is based out of our global operations center in Tampa, FL.

General Description

We are seeking a Business Analytics and Recognition Data Analyst to join our team! The Business Analytics and Recognition Data Analyst play a key role in supporting the firm’s data driven marketing, recognition, and knowledge management initiatives. This position is responsible for the collection, validation, analysis, and strategic use of firm data that supports legal directories, accolades, survey submissions, and internal reporting. Working within the Marketing Department and collaborating, the analyst helps improve data governance, submission processes, and reporting through dashboards and analytics, while supporting knowledge management efforts that enhance firm visibility and strategic decision-making.

Key Responsibilities And Essential Job Functions

Follow best practices to support submissions for publication, client, industry, and government surveys; as well as directory and award nominations, ensuring timely and accurate delivery.
Support the calendaring of directory, publication, and survey submissions, implementing and improving the submission process for each unique publication, directory, survey, or accolade submission while helping improve submission workflows and documentation.
Collect, maintain, and report on firm demographic, financial and operational data to support engagement, marketing, and other business professionals, including the development of dashboards and reporting tools using Microsoft Power BI or similar platforms.
Perform data validation and quality control across submissions and internal datasets to ensure consistency, accuracy, and high-quality deliverables.
Integrate and verify firm financial, client matter and HR Systems data while maintaining strict confidentiality and compliance with all Firm privacy guidelines, data retention standards, and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) requirements.
Support development, integration and maintenance of firm submissions and experience databases under the direction of Business Analytics and Marketing leadership and in partnership with the Enterprise Project Management Office (EPMO) as well as other Business Services stakeholders
Maintain documentation of declined opportunities or submissions to avoid, including rationale and historical context.
Maintain and report firm data metrics and performance indicators, providing regular updates to Marketing, Engagement and Firm leadership.
Stay abreast of the information needs, workflow and preferences of users and stakeholders in order to improve ease of access to information and best-in-class data support.
Support the development of Business Analytics and Recognition Data training material, including show sheets and training manuals, ensuring accuracy of all instructional material.
Help develop improved reporting of RFPs, surveys, directories, publications, accolades, and other submissions.
Participate in Business Analytics and Recognition Data team meetings and collaborate across departments to promote centralized and accessible firm data.
Assist in creating analytical reports in order to identify problems, opportunities, and potential solutions in day-to-day business functions.
Work in collaboration with Marketing, Knowledge Management, Finance, Human Resources, IT, and Innovation teams to promote centralization of and easy access to data.
Expected to maintain a regular and predictable work schedule and full attention to and engagement in work activities on behalf of the firm during business hours unless otherwise approved or required by applicable law.
Special projects and duties as assigned.

Required Skills

Data Analysis and Legal Knowledge Management

Ability to analyze information, logically organize information, and integrate findings into work product.
Attention to detail with high level of accuracy in data entry.
Intermediate understanding of legal systems and concepts, with an awareness of the legal industry and current trends
Experience with knowledge management, including some understanding of meta data's role in information organization.
Experience analyzing and reporting on large data sets, with some ability to synthesize large quantities of data and to express complex concepts in a concise, business format.
Experience building and maintaining legal taxonomies.
Knowledge of data collection methods and competencies, including an understanding of information organization and collecting data towards decision making
Critical evaluation skills, including the ability to assess the credibility, currency, and validity of information sources.
Familiarity with APIs and data visualization tools
Ability to adopt new technologies quickly, including AI‑enabled workflows and tools.

Professionalism and Leadership

Commitment to strong service values with the ability to exhibit sound professional judgment, discretion, strategic thinking, independent problem solving and accountability.
Effective communication skills, including active listening, clear writing, professional speaking, cultural sensitivity, emotional intelligence, and civility.
Experience with time management, including an understanding of prioritization towards team and organizational goals.
Understanding of and proven commitment to relationship building, team building and collaboration
Future-focused outlook with some experience engaging stakeholders towards surfacing and communicating problems, challenges, and opportunities.
Self-awareness of knowledge gaps with personal effort toward continuing education
Team-minded and diplomatic, with an engagement in mentorship and knowledge sharing
Independently motivated

Practical Skills

Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint, or Microsoft 365
Experience with web-based database resources
Experience editing information in SharePoint, ServiceNow, or similar.
Ability to adapt and apply new technologies and workflows.
Demonstrated stress management skills and effective prioritization of workload.
Ability to adapt in a fast-paced, collaborative work environment.
Basic troubleshooting skills with common IT issues involving access to databases and online content.

Required Qualifications & Education

Bachelor's Degree
Minimum of 2 years legal, corporate, or industry data experience or survey submission experience required; preferably in a law firm setting or equivalent.

Physical Requirements

Ability to sit or stand for extended periods of time.
Moderate or advanced keyboard usage.

Benefits:
Our goal is to promote a work environment in which individuals have access to the resources they need to be their best both professionally and personally, which includes resources that encourage individuals to focus on their health and well-being.

Below are the benefits we offer: comprehensive medical (PPO and HDHPs), dental and vision plans including coverage for domestic partners; life and AD&D insurance; short and long term disability insurance; tax-advantaged accounts for health care expenses, including FSAs and HSAs; FSAs for dependent care; health advocacy services; behavioral health and counseling resources for all family members; 401(k); profit sharing; backup dependent care; senior care planning support; resources for individuals with development disabilities and their caregivers; and paid holidays and other paid time off, including paid leave for new parents.

jobs.company: New York State Office of Mental Health
jobs.title: Data Analyst 2; Central Office
jobs.description:
Duties Description

Under the direction of the Data Analyst 3 and Business Systems Analyst 4, the Data Analyst 2s will conduct data analysis tasks, which may include data cleaning, data transformation, data modeling, and data visualization.

Specific duties include, but are not limited to, the following:

Assist in generating reports and creating visualizations to present data analysis findings. This includes organizing and formatting data in a clear and concise manner, creating charts and graphs, and summarizing key findings for presentation to stakeholders.
Responsible for collecting and integrating clinical and behavioral health data from various sources, such as electronic health records (EHRs), patient surveys, and behavioral health assessment tools. This may involve working with data extraction tools, cleaning, and transforming data, and ensuring data quality and integrity.
Assist in the coordination and implementation of United State Core Data for Interoperability (USCDI) Data Sets across OMH’s larger application portfolio.
Ensure the accuracy and integrity of data by performing data quality checks and validations. This includes identifying and resolving data inconsistencies, conducting data audits, and implementing data quality improvement measures.
Assist in integrating data from different sources to create a unified view of the data. This involves working with data integration tools and platforms, mapping data fields, and ensuring data compatibility and consistency.
Assist in the analysis of clinical and behavioral health data to evaluate the effectiveness of interventions, treatment plans, and programs. This involves measuring clinical outcomes, monitoring patient progress, and identifying areas for improvement in patient care and treatment outcomes.
Report to and collaborate with team members to understand data requirements, discuss analysis approaches, and share insights and findings.
Collaborate with healthcare professionals, including clinicians, psychologists, and behavioral health specialists, to understand their data analysis needs and provide them with relevant insights. This may involve participating in multidisciplinary team meetings and working closely with stakeholders to address their data-related questions and concerns.
Complete documentation on data analysis processes, methodologies, and findings for future reference. This includes maintaining documentation of data sources, data transformations, and analysis steps to ensure reproducibility and transparency.
Provide support the DMI in any priority data analysis activities. This includes assisting in data collection and preparation, conducting preliminary analysis, and providing insights and recommendations based on your analysis.

Minimum Qualifications

Non-competitive: seven years of experience* in data visualization, and statistical models and data mining tools.

Substitution: An associate’s degree may substitute for two years of general experience; or a bachelor’s degree may substitute for four years of general experience. A master’s degree in data or computer science, informatics, economics, statistics, mathematics, or a related field, may substitute for one year of general experience; or a Ph.D. in these fields may substitute for two years of general experience.

Additional Comments

The Mission of the New York State Office of Mental Health is to promote the mental health of all New Yorkers, with a focus on providing hope and supporting recovery for adults with serious mental illness and children with serious emotional disturbances. Applicants with lived mental health experience are encouraged to apply. OMH is deeply committed to supporting underserved individuals, organizations, and communities. To this end, OMH is focused on implementing activities and initiatives to reduce disparities in access, quality, and treatment outcomes for underserved populations. A critical component of these efforts is ensuring OMH is a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees' unique attributes and skills are valued and utilized to support the mission of the Agency. OMH is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer.

Specific scheduled hours and telecommuting availability for this position will be discussed during the interview process. Please note, employees are required to apply and obtain approval through management to telecommute according to operational needs and Agency Telecommuting Program Guidelines.

jobs.description:

Full-time

Description

Location: NAS Patuxent River, MD

Security Clearance Requirement: TS/SCI

Telework Eligible? No - 100% On-Site

Join our team supporting the U.S.!! Navy's operational testing of the E-2D Advanced Hawkeye aircraft. As a Data Analyst
, you will work with engineers, pilots, and military operators to collect, organize, analyze, and interpret test data that helps determine whether aircraft systems are operationally effective and suitable for the Fleet. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, working with large datasets, and turning data into meaningful insights that influence real-world decisions.

Key Responsibilities:

Test Planning Support (Integrated Evaluation Framework Development)

Assist in the development of Critical Operational Issues (COI) for operational assessments and evaluations.
Assist in the development of test design framework, in conjunction with mission-based COIs.
Support the development of data collection plans through research and analysis to evaluate operational effectiveness and suitability.

Operational Test Exercise Design

Devise test matrices and procedures for Integrated Testing and Operational Test (OT).
Determine data collection/reduction/reconstruction requirements.
Assist in designing and planning test operations in support Operational Test (OT).
Observe test events to collect test data and provide technical reports.

Test Operations Evaluation Support

Assist in briefing test/exercise participants, emphasizing the event objectives and specific data-gathering requirements for each participant.
Observe test events to collect test data and provide technical reports.

Test Analysis and Reporting

Perform data reduction and analysis of data to ensure data validity, prior to more detailed data processing.
Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, uncover insights, and answer key operational performance questions.
Correlate data using statistical techniques to analyze operational effectiveness and suitability tests.
Apply analytical techniques and critical thinking to explore data, identify patterns to support informed decision-making.
Assist in the development of deficiency reports (blue/gold sheets).
Assist in the development of draft test reports.

This description outlines the general nature and scope of the role. Additional duties may be assigned as necessary.

Requirements

Education and Experience:

Bachelor’s degree in a STEM area of study, required.
Must have 5 years of experience performing data analysis in a Test and Evaluation (T&E) environment, including the collection, validation, interpretation, and reporting of test data to support system performance assessments.
Familiarity with analytic tools such as JMP, MATLAB and R Studio, highly desired.
Operational test experience and DoD Acquisition experience, desired.
Operational experience in Navy aircraft, desired.

Ability to build positive, collaborative relationships across teams and with external partners.

jobs.description:
Job Description

Dewberry is seeking an MS4 Environmental Scientist for our Baltimore (Owings Mills), MD office. This position will support a growing water resources practice in the areas of National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System Municipal Separate Storm Sewer System (NPDES MS4) and Total Maximum Daily Load (TMDL) compliance. Tasks include MS4 permit compliance activities (illicit discharge inspections, BMP inspections, watershed assessment and planning); infrastructure resilience and mitigation planning; hydrologic and hydraulic analysis; stormwater design; stream restoration planning and design; and GIS work to support federal, state and local clients in the Mid-Atlantic region.

This is an excellent career opportunity for an enthusiastic and talented individual to join an exciting team of professionals. The position offers great potential for professional growth in a fantastic working environment and the opportunity to apply the latest technology to environmental and water resource projects.

Dewberry is a leading, market-facing professional services firm with more than 60 locations and 2,500 professionals nationwide. What sets us apart from our competitors are our people. At Dewberry, we seek out exceptional talent and strive to deliver the highest quality of services to our clients. Whether you’re an experienced professional or a new graduate, you’ll have the chance to collaborate with the best and brightest and work on innovative and complex projects at the forefront of the industry. Our commitment to excellence stems from our personal integrity and from other defining attributes, which we call “Dewberry at Work,” that have inspired our employees to be successful for more than a half-century.

Responsibilities

Performs NPDES MS4 compliance tasks.
Performs watershed restoration planning and design.
Performs Chesapeake Bay TMDL related planning and design.
Conducts stream assessments and stream restoration planning.
Contributes technically on regulatory compliance activities.
Conducts watershed assessment, management, planning and design.
Prepares habitat, ecosystem, and wetland assessments.
Performs hydrologic, hydraulic and water quality studies.
Performs geospatial data analysis in support of water resource planning tasks.
Must be willing/able to perform field work.

Required Skills

Required Skills & Required Experience

Bachelor’s degree in environmental science, environmental engineering, civil engineering, water resources or related field is required.
Hands-on experience with environmental assessment processes and ArcGIS required.
Experience with MicroStation is not required but preferred.
2-4 years of experience.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Must be able to perform in a collaborative environment.
Must be able to work independently.

Preferred Skills/Certifications

Knowledge of NPDES MS4/TMDL.
Experience with environmental permitting and planning.
Experience with the MDE and other local, state and federal permitting agencies.
Knowledge of stormwater and environmental regulations and permitting.
Knowledge or experience in project design/management.

*The salary range for this position will be $62,000 to $78,000*

At this time, Dewberry will not sponsor a new applicant for work authorization.
Dewberry is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status.
Only recipients of an offer of employment from Dewberry, will be required to submit to a background screening which may include, but is not limited to, employment verification, educational and other credential verification, driving record check, criminal background check, and an investigative consumer report. These screenings will be conducted by Dewberry’s background vendor of choice and will be conducted in compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local law.

jobs.title: Healthcare Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Extraordinary Careers. Endless Possibilities.

With the nation’s largest home infusion provider, there is no limit to the growth of your career.

Option Care Health, Inc. is the largest independent home and alternate site infusion services provider in the United States. With over 8,000 team members including 5,000 clinicians, we work compassionately to elevate standards of care for patients with acute and chronic conditions in all 50 states. Through our clinical leadership, expertise and national scale, Option Care Health is re-imagining the infusion care experience for patients, customers and team members.

Join a company that is taking action to develop an inclusive, respectful, engaging and rewarding culture for all team members. At Option Care Health your voice is heard, your work is valued, and you’re empowered to grow. Cultivating a team with a variety of talents, backgrounds and perspectives makes us stronger, innovative, and more impactful. Our organization requires extraordinary people to provide extraordinary care, so we are investing in a culture that attracts, hires and retains the best and brightest talent in healthcare.

Job Description Summary

Provides value-added analysis for the organization’s medical and pharmacy data and trends. Develops and maintains a thorough understanding of business processes at Option Care. Analyzes data to develop cost benefit analysis of proposed programs through collection and data analysis. Works on projects in market analysis, cost control analysis, dispensing, purchasing and other relevant analysis in relation to business needs.

Job Description

Job Responsibilities (listed in order of importance and/or time spent)

Develops and maintains a comprehensive understanding of business processes and industry practices.
Investigates key business problems through quantitative analyses and interpretation of utilization and healthcare cost data.
Translates specific business issues and questions into appropriate functional and analytic results.
Provides recommendations to support critical business decisions through data analyses
Identifies and evaluates emerging trends for internal and external clients by analyzing client data.
Monitors and studies operational, financial, clinical and utilization data to identify trends and communicate findings and recommendations to the leadership team.
Develops and presents cost-benefit analyses of proposed programs and makes specific recommendations tied to the business goals of the Company.
Analyzes and creates ad hoc analyses based on detailed data sets.
Collaborates and communicates complex analyses and findings in a simple, concise, and understandable manner across various functional areas.

Supervisory Responsibilities

None

Basic Education And/or Requirements

Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field (Information Science, Computer Science, Actuarial Science Finance or Math); Master’s degree preferred.
3 years of minimum healthcare analytic role experience
3 years of intermediate SQL, Microsoft Office such as Excel (formulas, calculations, charts, graphs, pivot tables, etc.), Visio, and PowerPoint skills

Basic Qualifications

Ability to meet frequently tight deadlines and to produce accurate appropriate analyses.
Strong aptitude for quickly troubleshooting and identifying the cause of questionable results within data and analyses.
Ability to work independently and effectively in a team.
Understanding of health care terminology and knowledgeable/experienced with provider data, claims data (pharmacy & medical), lab data, financial and enrollment data.
Possess outstanding oral and written communication skills
Advanced programming skills (e.g., Python and R), model development, regression analysis, and proficiency with advanced analytics and AI tools.

This job description is to be used as a guide for accomplishing Company and department objectives, and only covers the primary functions and responsibilities of the position. It is in no way to be construed as an all-encompassing list of duties.

Due to state pay transparency laws, the full range for the position is below:

Salary to be determined by the applicant's education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data.

Pay Range is $70,218.51-$117,008.35

Benefits

Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance
Paid Time off
Bonding Time Off
401K Retirement Savings Plan with Company Match
HSA Company Match
Flexible Spending Accounts
Tuition Reimbursement
myFlexPay
Family Support
Mental Health Services
Company Paid Life Insurance
Award/Recognition Programs

jobs.title: Data Analyst - Water Infrastructure
jobs.description:
Ardurra is seeking a Data Analyst to join our Public Works team in Houston, TX!

Ardurra is a multidisciplinary civil engineering services firm providing innovative infrastructure solutions to public and private sector clients throughout the United States. We leverage our technical expertise, integrated project delivery, and local market knowledge to deliver practical, sustainable solutions for public infrastructure, utilities, transportation, drainage, and community development projects.

Our Houston Public Works Infrastructure team partners with municipalities, local governments, utility providers, and public agencies to plan, evaluate, and improve critical infrastructure systems. We support projects involving utility master planning, capital improvement programs, infrastructure resiliency, system capacity evaluations, drainage and utility assessments, and long-range infrastructure planning.

Primary Function

The Data Analyst (Level I or II) will support water and wastewater infrastructure projects through the application of engineering principles, data analytics, and visualization techniques. This role will support hydraulic modeling efforts, develop dashboards and analytical tools, automate workflows, and transform complex infrastructure data into actionable insights for engineers, project managers, and utility clients.

The ideal candidate will possess an engineering background combined with strong analytical and programming skills, a passion for learning, and an interest in applying data science techniques to municipal infrastructure challenges.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and data visualization tools for infrastructure and utility projects.
Analyze engineering datasets and identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for operational improvements.
Support hydraulic modeling and system analysis efforts for water and wastewater infrastructure projects.
Assist with model calibration and interpretation of hydraulic modeling results.
Utilize Python, SQL, and other analytical tools to automate workflows and process large datasets.
Work with GIS, SCADA, and other infrastructure datasets.
Support capital improvement planning, asset management, and infrastructure resiliency initiatives.
Collaborate with engineers, project managers, and technical staff to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Continuously learn and apply emerging technologies and data science methodologies to engineering challenges.

Required

Education and Experience Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, Chemical Engineering, or related engineering discipline from an ABET-accredited university, or an equivalent quantitative, data science, or computer science background with relevant infrastructure experience.
Minimum of 1 year (Level I) or 3 years (Level II) of experience in engineering, analytics, or infrastructure-related data analysis.
Demonstrated understanding of data science concepts, statistical methods, or analytical programming through education or professional experience.
Experience utilizing Python and/or SQL for data analysis and automation.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
An experienced AI worker with strong judgment about when and which AI tools are appropriate, and a track record of integrating them effectively and responsibly into technical work.
Working understanding of machine learning concepts and methods, and how they apply to data analysis problems.

Preferred

Engineer-in-Training (EIT) certification.
Master's degree in Data Science, Engineering, Water Resources, or related field.
Experience with water/wastewater hydraulic modeling.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization platforms.
Experience with GIS platforms and geospatial data.
Experience with SCADA data, operational data, or utility system datasets preferred.
Familiarity with AI tools and Large Language Models.
Understanding of how machine learning applies in a water or wastewater context (e.g., demand forecasting, anomaly detection, asset condition prediction).
Experience within municipal infrastructure, public works, or water/wastewater environments.
Strong desire to learn, grow, and develop new technical skills.
Data engineering, automation, cloud infrastructure, database engineering, analytics, or machine learning certificates from online learning platforms such as Coursera, Codecademy, or Udemy.

Why Ardurra?

While Ardurra offers competitive compensation and rich benefits programs, it is our culture that truly sets us apart from our peers. We nurture a family-like culture, striving to create a work environment that is enjoyable, challenging and rewarding but also fun. We are acutely focused on developing our staff, whether through our internal Ardurra Academy or through our industry-leading Leadership program. We have made a deliberate and focused commitment to nurture a people-centric culture where people are: valued as individuals; supported in their professional and career development with multiple, varied career paths; provided the tools and resources to be successful, engaged, and satisfied in their work; and positive benefits, time-off programs, and flexibility to help maintain a healthy balance between work and home.

Ardurra is an Equal Opportunity/ Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, gender identity or sexual orientation.

NOTICE TO THIRD PARTY AGENCIES:

Ardurra does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of a signed Agency Agreement, Ardurra will not consider or agree to payment of any referral compensation or recruiter fee. If a resume or candidate is submitted to any hiring manager without a previously signed agreement, Ardurra reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. These candidates will be considered property of Ardurra. We’re not currently looking to add any more agencies to our list of approved vendors, so please do not contact any of our managers or recruitment team with sales calls or details of your candidates.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Koniag Professional Services, LLC, a Koniag Government Services company, is seeking a Data Analyst to support KPS and our government customer in Dallas, TX. This position requires the candidate to be able to obtain a Public Trust. This position is for a Future New Business Opportunity.

We offer competitive compensation and an extraordinary benefits package including health, dental and vision insurance, 401K with company matching, flexible spending accounts, paid holidays, three weeks paid time off, and more.

The Data Analyst will collect, analyze, and report on program data to identify trends, support decision-making, and drive process improvements. Core responsibilities include data quality oversight, system maintenance, and conducting quantitative and qualitative analyses to develop reports, visualizations, and data-driven insights for federal staff and program leadership. This is an ONSITE position required to be performed at the Dallas office locations, with telework available upon approval.

Essential Functions, Responsibilities & Duties may include, but are not limited to:

Compile, prepare, and analyze quantitative data using Excel, Python, R, and other analytical tools to support grant administration and program oversight.
Conduct both basic and complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights across federal grant programs.
Perform longitudinal data analysis, including Program Information Report (PIR) analysis, and develop high-quality written reports explaining the results of data compilation and analysis activities.
Prepare management information reports on a periodic, cyclical, or ad-hoc basis to support program leadership and decision-making.
Identify and resolve reporting anomalies and data quality issues to ensure the integrity of program data.
Create data-driven presentations for management meetings, conferences, and leadership briefings.
Support congressional reporting and respond to other high-priority data requests in a timely and accurate manner.
Conduct trend analysis and comparative assessments across programs and regions to identify opportunities for program improvement.
Conduct qualitative analysis of grant applications, budgets, intake forms, and other grant-related documents.
Review documentation to identify errors, missing information, or required follow-up actions and communicate findings appropriately.
Analyze correspondence and communications between the federal government and grant recipients to identify themes and patterns in textual data.
Leverage AI tools appropriately while adhering to federal policies, including approved AI platforms.
Synthesize qualitative findings into actionable recommendations for program staff and leadership.
Develop compelling data stories that communicate complex insights to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Create executive summaries and briefing materials for leadership, including materials for state conferences and internal decision-making support.
Design and deliver oral presentations with support materials to a variety of audiences.
Generate crosstabs, detailed analytical tables, and data visualizations to support reporting needs.
Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable recommendations that drive program improvements.

Education

Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Data Science, or a related field from an accredited college or university.

Required Experience

A minimum of 3 years of relevant post-baccalaureate professional experience directly related to data analysis.

Required Skills And Competencies

Strong analytical and critical-thinking skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
Demonstrated ability to aggregate, analyze, and present data from multiple sources in a clear and meaningful way.
Experience with methodology or implementation of AI application tools (GitHub, R, Qualtrics, Tableau, or Power BI), ArcGIS
Experience reporting analytical findings to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience with Excel, Python, R, and preferably Smartsheet
Experience working with data layers and data infrastructure to support analytical and reporting activities.
Experience with qualitative data analysis methodologies, including identifying themes and patterns in textual data.
Proven ability to manage multiple analytical projects simultaneously and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and web-based collaboration tools.
Professional communication skills, both written and oral, include the ability to produce high-quality written reports and deliver oral presentations.

Travel Requirements

Contractor personnel are required to travel within and outside the Region during the performance of this contract to conduct site visits, attend meetings, conferences, and training

Additional Requirements

U.S. citizenship or legal authorization to work in the United States
Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance
Ability to pass required background investigations for access to federal facilities and systems
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment
Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately
Willingness to complete mandatory training requirements

Security Requirement

Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance

Desired Skills And Competencies

Experience working with federal grant programs, particularly in data analysis or program oversight capacity.
Knowledge of Head Start or similar early childhood education programs.
SQL, GIS, Data Visualizations, Survey Methodology or Implementation, application of AI
Knowledge of survey methodology or survey implementation practices.
Experience with AI tools and platforms, including GitHub and Qualtrics, in compliance with applicable federal policies.
Familiarity with web-based data quality and collaboration tools.

Our Equal Employment Opportunity Policy

The company is an equal opportunity employer. The company shall not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, color, religion, creed, ethnicity, sex, sexual orientation, gender or gender identity (except where gender is a bona fide occupational qualification), national origin or ancestry, age, disability, citizenship, military/veteran status, marital status, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. We are committed to equal employment opportunity in all decisions related to employment, promotion, wages, benefits, and all other privileges, terms, and conditions of employment.

The company is dedicated to seeking all qualified applicants. If you require an accommodation to navigate or apply for a position on our website, please get in touch with Heaven Wood via e-mail at accommodations@koniag-gs.com or by calling 703-488-9377 to request accommodations.

Koniag Government Services (KGS) is an Alaska Native Owned corporation supporting the values and traditions of our native communities through an agile employee and corporate culture that delivers Enterprise Solutions, Professional Services and Operational Management to Federal Government Agencies. As a wholly owned subsidiary of Koniag, we apply our proven commercial solutions to a deep knowledge of Defense and Civilian missions to provide forward leaning technical, professional, and operational solutions. KGS enables successful mission outcomes for our customers through solution-oriented business partnerships and a commitment to exceptional service delivery. We ensure long-term success with a continuous improvement approach while balancing the collective interests of our customers, employees, and native communities. For more information, please visit www.koniag-gs.com.

Equal Opportunity Employer/Veterans/Disabled. Shareholder Preference in accordance with Public Law 88-352

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Koniag Professional Services, LLC, a Koniag Government Services company, is seeking a Data Analyst to support KPS and our government customer in Kansas City, MO. This position requires the candidate to be able to obtain a Public Trust. This position is for a Future New Business Opportunity.

We offer competitive compensation and an extraordinary benefits package including health, dental and vision insurance, 401K with company matching, flexible spending accounts, paid holidays, three weeks paid time off, and more

The Data Analyst will collect, analyze, and report on program data to identify trends, support decision-making, and drive process improvements. Core responsibilities include data quality oversight, system maintenance, and conducting quantitative and qualitative analyses to develop reports, visualizations, and data-driven insights for federal staff and program leadership. This is an ONSITE position required to be performed at the Kansa City office locations, with telework available upon approval.

Essential Functions, Responsibilities & Duties may include, but are not limited to:

Compile, prepare, and analyze quantitative data using Excel, Python, R, and other analytical tools to support grant administration and program oversight.
Conduct both basic and complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights across federal grant programs.
Perform longitudinal data analysis, including Program Information Report (PIR) analysis, and develop high-quality written reports explaining the results of data compilation and analysis activities.
Prepare management information reports on a periodic, cyclical, or ad-hoc basis to support program leadership and decision-making.
Identify and resolve reporting anomalies and data quality issues to ensure the integrity of program data.
Create data-driven presentations for management meetings, conferences, and leadership briefings.
Support congressional reporting and respond to other high-priority data requests in a timely and accurate manner.
Conduct trend analysis and comparative assessments across programs and regions to identify opportunities for program improvement.
Conduct qualitative analysis of grant applications, budgets, intake forms, and other grant-related documents.
Review documentation to identify errors, missing information, or required follow-up actions and communicate findings appropriately.
Analyze correspondence and communications between the federal government and grant recipients to identify themes and patterns in textual data.
Leverage AI tools appropriately while adhering to federal policies, including approved AI platforms.
Synthesize qualitative findings into actionable recommendations for program staff and leadership.
Develop compelling data stories that communicate complex insights to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Create executive summaries and briefing materials for leadership, including materials for state conferences and internal decision-making support.
Design and deliver oral presentations with support materials to a variety of audiences.
Generate crosstabs, detailed analytical tables, and data visualizations to support reporting needs.
Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable recommendations that drive program improvements.

Education

Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Data Science, or a related field from an accredited college or university.

Required Experience

A minimum of 3 years of relevant post-baccalaureate professional experience directly related to data analysis.

Required Skills And Competencies

Strong analytical and critical-thinking skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
Demonstrated ability to aggregate, analyze, and present data from multiple sources in a clear and meaningful way.
Experience with methodology or implementation of AI application tools (GitHub, R, Qualtrics, Tableau, or Power BI), ArcGIS
Experience reporting analytical findings to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience with Excel, Python, R, and preferably Smartsheet
Experience working with data layers and data infrastructure to support analytical and reporting activities.
Experience with qualitative data analysis methodologies, including identifying themes and patterns in textual data.
Proven ability to manage multiple analytical projects simultaneously and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and web-based collaboration tools.
Professional communication skills, both written and oral, include the ability to produce high-quality written reports and deliver oral presentations.

Travel Requirements

Contractor personnel are required to travel within and outside the Region during the performance of this contract to conduct site visits, attend meetings, conferences, and training

Additional Requirements

U.S. citizenship or legal authorization to work in the United States
Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance
Ability to pass required background investigations for access to federal facilities and systems
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment
Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately
Willingness to complete mandatory training requirements

Security Requirement

Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance

Desired Skills And Competencies

Experience working with federal grant programs, particularly in data analysis or program oversight capacity.
Knowledge of Head Start or similar early childhood education programs.
SQL, GIS, Data Visualizations, Survey Methodology or Implementation, application of AI
Knowledge of survey methodology or survey implementation practices.
Experience with AI tools and platforms, including GitHub and Qualtrics, in compliance with applicable federal policies.
Familiarity with web-based data quality and collaboration tools.

jobs.title: Exploration Geologist
jobs.description:
Exploration Geologist

Winnemucca, NV

$25 - $35/hr

Our client is an established gold producer operating a growing mining operation in northern Nevada, one of the world's premier mining jurisdictions. With active exploration programs supporting both near-mine resource growth and long-term project development, they are seeking an Exploration Geologist for a 3 month contract to support their exploration success.
This is an excellent opportunity for a geologist or experienced geological technician who enjoys hands-on core logging, geological interpretation, and working closely with exploration teams.

PERKS & BENEFITS

Compensation $25 - $35/hr
Join an active exploration program with significant geological potential
Work alongside experienced geologists and technical professionals
Gain valuable exposure to drill programs, geological interpretation, and exploration workflows
Opportunity to contribute directly to project advancement and discovery
Accommodations, and travel support provided where applicable
Collaborative team environment with a strong focus on safety
Potential for contract extensions and future exploration opportunities

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

Log drill core to company standards, including lithology, mineralization, alteration, structure, recovery, and geotechnical characteristics
Select, mark, and manage sample intervals while ensuring adherence to QA/QC and sampling protocols
Collect, label, package, and track samples for laboratory submission while maintaining chain-of-custody procedures
Maintain accurate digital and physical geological records, including logging databases, sample sheets, and photo documentation
Capture and organize high-quality core photography and assist with geological data management
Support geological interpretation through accurate data collection and communication with senior geologists and exploration teams

WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TABLE

Experience logging drill core in a mineral exploration, mining, or geotechnical environment.
Comfort working with geological databases, digital logging systems, and field technology.
A proactive, safety-focused approach and the ability to work effectively within a collaborative exploration team.
Degree or diploma in Geology, Earth Sciences, Geological Technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.

ABOUT US:

TPD is a leading workforce solutions provider with a mission to help individuals succeed by providing access to opportunities in jobs, careers, and professional development. A niche provider serving North America, we specialize in placing skilled candidates within the Manufacturing, Semiconductor, and Mining industries. Whether you seek flexible or permanent positions, we assist in starting, changing, or advancing careers in a way that aligns with your goals.
We are an Equal Employment Opportunity employer that values the strength diversity brings to the workplace. jobs.title: Field Technician
jobs.description:
Overview

At UES, we’re a team of more than 4,000 engineers, scientists, geologists, inspectors, technicians, and drillers united by a shared purpose—to make a meaningful impact in the communities we serve. As a national leader in geotechnical engineering, environmental consulting, and materials testing and inspection, we collaborate on transformative projects across transportation, energy, water, healthcare, and more. Learn more about the benefits of joining Team UES and our core values at careers.teamues.com.

Position Overview

The Field Technician is responsible for performing field inspections, testing, and data collection related to foundation construction and geotechnical engineering. Working under the supervision of senior technicians and engineers, the technician ensures compliance with project specifications, industry standards, and safety requirements.This role involves site work, detailed documentation, and collaboration with construction and engineering teams to help ensure the safe and proper installation of foundation systems.

Responsibilities

Perform field observations, testing, and documentation during the installation of foundation systems
Collect and log field data including foundation depths, installation torque or blow counts, concrete volumes, rebar placement, and alignment.
Monitor construction activities to ensure they conform to project specifications and geotechnical recommendations.
Conduct on-site testing such as concrete sampling, slump tests, air content, and temperature measurements during foundation pours.
Document findings and prepare accurate and timely field reports, logs, and daily summaries.
Communicate effectively with contractors, engineers, and field supervisors to report progress and identify any non-conformance or issues.
Ensure all equipment used is calibrated, maintained, and operated according to company standards.
Follow project schedules and meet daily site responsibilities with flexibility to adapt to changing conditions.
Adhere to all site-specific and company-wide safety protocols, including the use of required PPE.
Participate in training, certification, and development opportunities to enhance technical knowledge and skills.
Support other field-testing duties as needed for geotechnical and construction materials testing projects.
Other duties as assigned

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent required; vocational training or coursework in construction, engineering, or geotechnical disciplines preferred.
0-3 years of experience in a field-based or foundation-related construction preferred
Valid driver’s license with an acceptable driving record; ability to operate company-assigned vehicles safely.
Industry certifications such as ACI Level 1 Concrete Testing, or ability to obtain within a specified time period.
Commitment to workplace safety, following OSHA standards and company safety policies, including the proper use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
Familiarity with common foundation construction methods and terminology is a plus.
Basic computer proficiency, including experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) and Field Data Collection (FDC) software or similar industry tools.
Mathematical skills sufficient to add, subtract, multiply, and divide into all units of measure using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals.
Ability to read, write, and interpret blueprints, procedures, manuals, technical documents, and reports related to drilling, safety, and project requirements.
Ability to perform physical tasks, including lifting and carrying equipment weighing up to 50 lbs., standing for extended periods, and working in various weather and environmental conditions.
Efficient time management, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to complete tasks efficiently and accurately.
Attention to detail to ensure accurate sample collection, testing procedures, and documentation.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively, demonstrating a strong work ethic and commitment to producing high-quality results.
Flexibility to work varying schedules, including early mornings, weekends, overtime, and potential overnight travel as project demands require.

Travel Requirements

This position requires extensive travel, approximately up to 70% of the time, in support of business

objectives and client engagements. Travel may be both scheduled and on short notice, depending on

project needs. Destinations typically include job sites, field locations, or regional offices, where work may

be conducted in outdoor or operational environments. Adherence to all company safety protocols and

use of PPE is required during travel and on-site work .

Physical Demands & Work Environment

This position primarily works in an outdoor work environment, regularly exposed to varying field conditions, including:
Sites that may not be accessible by vehicle, requiring travel on foot through wetlands, swamps, grasslands, fields, and other uneven or rugged terrain with natural and man-made obstacles such as sand, brush, and debris.
Moderate to loud noise levels, common in active construction and industrial environments.
Exposure to environmental elements including dirt, dust, sun, heat, humidity, and adverse weather conditions.

The physical requirements for this position may include and are not limited to:
Ability to lift and/or carry 35 to 75 lbs. up to 75 feet, involving regular repetitive motion.
Ability to stand, walk, drive, and operate equipment for extended periods.
Frequent use of hands and arms for typing, handling tools, and operating equipment.
Ability to bend, twist, reach, stoop, pull, kneel, climb, balance, lift, carry, and push as required for fieldwork and inspections.
Ability to speak and hear effectively to communicate in noisy environments and with project teams.
Ability to use your senses to see (including close, distant, and peripheral vison, depth vision, and ability to focus) smell

EEO Statement

UES is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is proud to recruit the most qualified candidates. Please see our full EEO Statement at the bottom of the page
here

In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways:

Assisting with the design, testing, deployment, and ongoing management of digital employees and AI‑enabled tools that support legal workflows, including contracting and negotiations.

Performing data analytics and reporting to support transactional work, including extracting, analysing, and presenting insights on contracts and trends, and partnering with attorneys and other stakeholders to maintain and enhance contracting‑related datasets, dashboards, and reporting used for management, risk, and strategic decision‑making.
Supporting the administration, optimization, and adoption of legal technology platforms, including CLM systems, contract repositories, analytics tools, and AI‑driven legal solutions.
Helping to develop, document, and maintain processes, controls, and playbooks relating to AI use, data governance, and legal operations.
Communicating and coordinating with a broad range of internal and external stakeholders, including business teams, sourcing, and external counsel, to support contracting processes, vendor management, data quality, and related governance initiatives.

To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following:

Experience in a paralegal, analyst, or legal operations role, preferably within a corporate or financial services environment.
Strong data analytics skills, including experience working with structured and unstructured data, spreadsheets, reporting tools, dashboards, or legal analytics platforms.
Demonstrated experience or strong interest in AI, automation, and advanced legal technology.
Experience supporting contracting, negotiations, or third-party governance is strongly preferred.
High degree of comfort working with technology platforms and digital workflows.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.

Effective communicator and collaborator.
Curious, proactive, and motivated to contribute to legal innovation.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Role: Data Analyst

Location: Phoenix, Arizona, United States

Experience Required - 8+ Years

Must Have Technical/Functional Skills

SQL , Data Analysis & Analytics, Data Governance, Data Quality Management, Metadata & Data Lineage Management, Reporting & Visualization, Cloud Data Platforms(GCP), Data Integration & ETL, AI/ML Fundamentals

The Role

As a Data Analyst / Data Management & Governance Professional with Emerging AI Experience, you will play a key role in analyzing, managing, and governing financial data while supporting the development and implementation of AI-enabled solutions. You will collaborate with engineers, product managers, data stewards, and business stakeholders to ensure high-quality, reliable, and compliant data assets that drive business decisions and customer-facing solutions.

This role provides opportunities to deepen your expertise in data analytics, data management, data governance, reporting, regulatory compliance, and cloud-based data platforms, while gaining hands-on exposure to Artificial Intelligence (AI), Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and Agentic AI technologies. Working within a highly regulated financial environment, you will contribute to building scalable data solutions and support AI initiatives under the guidance of experienced engineers and data leaders.

Responsibilities

• Analyze and interpret large financial datasets to generate actionable insights, support decision-making, and improve business outcomes.
• Support data management initiatives, including data quality, data lineage, metadata management, master data management, and data lifecycle processes.
• Contribute to the implementation and maintenance of data governance frameworks, policies, standards, controls, and regulatory compliance requirements.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data accuracy, consistency, accessibility, and governance across enterprise data platforms.
• Assist in the development and maintenance of reporting solutions, dashboards, KPIs, and analytical models to support business and operational needs.
• Participate in cloud-based data platform initiatives, including data migration, integration, automation, and process optimization activities.
• Support AI and machine learning initiatives by preparing, validating, and governing datasets used for model development and deployment.
• Gain hands-on experience with AI technologies, including Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and Agentic AI solutions, while assisting in the development and testing of AI-powered features and workflows.
• Contribute to monitoring, evaluating, and improving data and AI solution performance, reliability, and compliance in production environments.
• Work closely with product managers, engineers, data stewards, and business stakeholders to translate business requirements into scalable data and analytics solutions.
• Support the documentation of data governance processes, data dictionaries, business rules, and operational procedures to ensure transparency and compliance.
• Participate in continuous improvement efforts focused on data quality, governance maturity, automation, and AI adoption across the organization.
Preferred Skills & Experience
• Experience in Data Analysis, Data Management, Data Governance, Data Quality, Reporting, and Regulatory Compliance.
• Knowledge of SQL, data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.), and cloud-based data platforms.
• Understanding of data governance concepts, including metadata management, data lineage, data stewardship, and data controls.
• Exposure to cloud technologies such as Google Cloud Platform (GCP), AWS, or Azure.
